title: Retro Tuna Mould Mousse

seo.title: Retro Tuna Mould Mousse - Marinda Kook

html:
My mom always made the fish mould for high days and holidays – and we thought it was wonderful. Then there came a stage in my life when I was almost a bit embarrassed by moulded dishes. Not anymore! I eat this fish mould with a song in my heart. I serve it to guests and they eat it and ask for the recipe. I usually make my fish dish in a ring pan or a copper fish mould or a bread tin. Today, though, I made a bunch of small individual moulds so each guest could get their own. The dish can serve as a main meal, starter or little snack with a crackers and a cold glass of something. Versatile is her name.

You can freeze the fish and take it out for that day when the unexpected visitor turns up.

 

YOU WILL NEED:

  • 3 tins tuna in water
  • 1 onion
  • 4 gherkins
  • 1 cup uncut parsley
  • 350 ml mayonnaise
  • 100 ml tomato sauce
  • 10 ml tabasco (you can leave it out)
  • 5 ml sugar
  • salt and pepper
  • 15 ml gelatine
  • 125 ml hot water

NOW:

  1. chop the onion, parsley and gherkins finely in a food processor or by hand
  2. drain the water off the fish and put the fish in a mixing bowl
  3. pour the hot water into a cup and sprinkle the gelatine over it
  4. put the tomato sauce, mayonnaise, sugar and tabasco in another bowl and stir it
  5. now stir the gelatine until it has dissolved in the water and add it to the mayonnaise mixture
  6. now combine everything and stir it through well
  7. spray your containers with spray and cook
  8. pour your fish mixture into the containers
  9. put it in the fridge and let it set

Choose your prettiest lettuce leaves and serve it with bread or crackers, and enjoy the healthy bite until you're well and truly satisfied.

Remember you can freeze it for those unexpected guests.
